_comment: sovereign-os surface-map dashboard (R493, R453+, R546). Visualizes the operator-§1g surface-map instrument that audits each module's coverage across the 8-surface §1g delivery ladder. Sourced from sovereign_os_operator_surface_map_query_total{verb,surface,result} (Layer B, SDD-016) emitted by scripts/operator/surface-map.py. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the future surface-map-dashboard waiver and registers surface-map in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard) — fully closing the 4-instrument compliance meta-coverage loop (surface-map now appears in its own surface-map and tracks queries against itself). R546 expands the dashboard verb-stat row with two additional cards (milestone + selfdef) mirroring the R540 + R544 ceiling-promotions on the MCP surface — operator-§1g coverage symmetry: every parameterless verb exposed via MCP now has a corresponding stat card on the dashboard.

panels.title: surfaces verb (count)
panels.description: Enumerates the 8-surface §1g delivery ladder (core / cli / tui / api / mcp / dashboard / webapp / service).

panels.title: coverage verb (count)
panels.description: Per-module coverage matrix — operator's primary 'how am I covered on the 8-surface §1g contract?' verb.

panels.title: gaps verb (count)
panels.description: Fail-loud surface-gap list — modules below threshold (3 surfaces) or with FUTURE-class waivers.

panels.title: waivers verb (count)
panels.description: Operator-blessed waivers inventory — every surface explicitly opted-out, anchored + justified (R478 structural vs FUTURE distinction).

panels.title: surface-map query rate per verb (5m)
panels.description: Per-verb query rate over time. surfaces / modules / coverage / gaps / waivers / selfdef — operator's surface-map-tooling usage pattern.

panels.title: surface-map surface attention (5m)
panels.description: Per-surface query rate. core / cli / tui / api / mcp / dashboard / webapp / service — operator-revealed-preference for which surface-axis is most-watched.

panels.title: surface-map result distribution
panels.description: ok / unknown-module / unknown-surface / issues — operator-§1g result-class breakdown across all verbs.

panels.title: surface-map current state (verb × surface × result)
panels.description: Per-(verb, surface, result) histogram — operator-discoverable: 'which surface am I exercising most, and is it passing?'

panels.title: milestone verb (count)
panels.description: R540 first-class §1g ceiling-closure rollup verb. Post-R539 historic milestone (TWELFTH §1g module at ceiling) this verb returns the system-wide rollup: total_modules + at_structural_ceiling_count + full_8_surface_count + zero_future_waivers. Surfaced over CLI (R540) + API /milestone (R541) + webapp panel (R542) + watch TUI banner (R543) + MCP `surface-map-milestone` (R541).

panels.title: selfdef verb (count)
panels.description: R462 cross-repo SurfaceManifest discovery verb — surfaces the §1g delivery state of sibling ecosystem repos (selfdef / info-hub / etc.) under /etc/selfdef/surfaces. Exposed over MCP via `surface-map-selfdef` (R544) for one-call cross-repo §1g visibility.

options.content:
**Operator §1g verbatim** (sacrosanct):

> "everything is not just core, not just cli, not just TUI, not just API, not just tool and MCP but also Dashboards and Web Apps and Services"

> "We do not minimize anything."

This instrument enforces the §1g 8-surface delivery contract: each operator-§1g module must ship across at least N surfaces (default threshold = 3), each remaining surface explicitly waived with an operator-rationalized note. surface-map is one of the 4 instruments aggregated by the `compliance` rollup (alongside doc-coverage / anti-minimization-audit / ux-design-audit).

**8-surface §1g delivery ladder (sacrosanct, R453):**

1. `core` — substrate logic (library / config / data-model)
2. `cli` — `sovereign-osctl` verb (operator's primary terminal surface)
3. `tui` — interactive refresh-loop surface (operator-watched dashboard)
4. `api` — REST / gRPC endpoint (machine-to-machine access)
5. `mcp` — Model Context Protocol surface (agent-accessible)
6. `dashboard` — Grafana / Prometheus visualization (operator-watched chart)
7. `webapp` — master-dashboard subpath (browser-discoverable surface)
8. `service` — systemd unit / daemon (long-running execution)

**Threshold:** ≥3 shipped surfaces per module. Below threshold = `surface-gap` anti-min pattern fires.

**R478 precision (structural vs FUTURE):** a `not applicable — ...` waiver is STRUCTURAL (at operator-stated ceiling); a `FUTURE — ...` waiver is ROADMAP (tracked gap). Only FUTURE-class waivers fire the `surface-gap` anti-min pattern (R478) — structural waivers are CORRECTLY-SHAPED work, not minimized work.

**Meta-coverage loop closed:** R493 registers surface-map ITSELF in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard). The instrument that audits surface coverage now appears in its own audit — the inspector inspects the inspector. This closes the symmetric loop initiated by R489 (compliance self-aggregator), R490 (anti-min self-scanner), R491 (doc-coverage self-doc), R492 (ux-design-audit self-audit).

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl surface-map surfaces                # enumerate 8
sovereign-osctl surface-map modules                 # list audited modules
sovereign-osctl surface-map coverage --module <m>   # per-module matrix
sovereign-osctl surface-map gaps                    # fail-loud gap list
sovereign-osctl surface-map waivers [--module <m>]  # waiver inventory
sovereign-osctl surface-map selfdef                 # cross-repo (selfdef)
```

**Metric:** `sovereign_os_operator_surface_map_query_total{verb,surface,result}` (SDD-016 Layer B)

**Source:** R453 shipped the 8-surface instrument + 6 verbs. R493 (R453+) ships THIS dashboard + registers surface-map as a first-class module in its own MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os surface-map
